top_signals.title: Wargaming for Improved Acquisition: What Does It Take?
top_signals.summary:
A few months ago, I attended a panel discussion for a wargame simulating rapid industrial mobilization for armed conflict. Conducted by a leading university, with teams composed of former senior defense officials, the game probed how government and industry collaboration would play out given minimal coordination before the onset of a crisis. On the panel, the defense leaders confessed how infrequently they engaged with industry in real life to plan for a national emergency. This declared lack of public-private planning for large-scale conflict matches what I’ve experienced as a defense planner and wargame developer: Outside of rhetorical claims at annual

The post Wargaming for Improved Acquisition: What Does It Take? appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: ‘They got very lucky,’ Trump says of downed Apache helicopter’s crew
top_signals.summary: After a U.S. Army Apache helicopter was downed by an Iranian drone, President Donald Trump said the rescued aviators “got very lucky.”

top_signals.title: NTT unit said to seek $1 billion to develop U.S. data centers
top_signals.summary: The company is working with Citigroup to raise the money by selling stakes in a development company for the new data center projects.

top_signals.title: US eyes Japanese funding for nuclear revival, Lutnick says
SpaceX stock is coming to Solana on the same day it lists on Nasdaq
The Reliability Crisis Utilities Don't Want to Talk About
top_signals.summary: What are the optics? AI centers are gobbling up power production, transmission space, and water. Transmission line expansion at a pitiable pace. A rush to build enough power plants. Forest fires burn lines and vice versa. The Trump administration plans to solve power shortages by cancelling windmill projects and subsidizing coal-fired power plants. (Why not quash the sales of electric vehicles by subsidizing horses? Just a question.) And, of course, in the meantime, pictures of utility executives at the EEI convention congratulating themselves…

top_signals.title: Why AI-driven threats are exposing the limits of MSP security stacks
top_signals.summary: AI-driven attacks are exposing the limits of fragmented MSP security stacks and slow response workflows. Kaseya breaks down why integrated security, automation, and recovery are becoming essential. [...]
